context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements Docker tagging rules (Build.BuildId as primary, also push latest, but deploy using only specific tag, never :latest in production), uses deployment jobs for environment tracking, and uses KubernetesManifest@0 properly.

checklist.name: Build.BuildId tag
checklist.description: Docker images are tagged with `$(Build.BuildId)` (or a variable holding it, like `$(tag)` where tag = $(Build.BuildId))

checklist.name: Also pushes latest tag
checklist.description: The Docker push step includes both the build-specific tag AND `latest` in the tags list

checklist.name: Deploy uses specific tag only
checklist.description: The Kubernetes deployment task references the specific build tag (e.g., $(tag)) and NOT `:latest` as the image tag

checklist.name: Deployment jobs used
checklist.description: Both staging and production deploy stages use `deployment:` jobs (not regular `job:`) with `environment:` specified

checklist.name: KubernetesManifest@0 task
checklist.description: The pipeline uses `KubernetesManifest@0` (pinned at @0) for Kubernetes deployments

checklist.name: Docker@2 task used
checklist.description: The Docker build/push step uses `Docker@2` (pinned at @2)

checklist.name: Pinned vmImage
checklist.description: Pool vmImage uses a specific version (e.g., ubuntu-22.04) not ubuntu-latest

checklist.name: displayName on all tasks
checklist.description: Every task and script step has a displayName property

checklist.name: dependsOn between stages
checklist.description: Deploy stages have `dependsOn` referencing prior stages

checklist.name: No @latest task usage
checklist.description: No task in the file uses @latest version
